2604.20658.title: Cooperative Profiles Predict Multi-Agent LLM Team Performance in AI for Science Workflows
2604.20658.paper_summary: Multi-agent systems built from teams of large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed for collaborative scientific reasoning and problem-solving. These systems require agents to coordinate under shared constraints, such as GPUs or credit balances, where cooperative behavior matters. Behavioral economics provides a rich toolkit of games that isolate distinct cooperation mechanisms, yet it remains unknown whether a model's behavior in these stylized settings predicts its performance in realistic collaborative tasks. Here, we benchmark 35 open-weight LLMs across six behavioral economics games and show that game-derived cooperative profiles robustly predict downstream performance in AI-for-Science tasks, where teams of LLM agents collaboratively analyze data, build models, and produce scientific reports under shared budget constraints. Models that effectively coordinate games and invest in multiplicative team production (rather than greedy strategies) produce better scientific reports across three outcomes, accuracy, quality, and completion. These associations hold after controlling for multiple factors, indicating that cooperative disposition is a distinct, measurable property of LLMs not reducible to general ability. Our behavioral games framework thus offers a fast and inexpensive diagnostic for screening cooperative fitness before costly multi-agent deployment.
2604.20658.paper_summary_zh: 多代理系統由大型語言模型（LLMs）團隊組成，越來越多地被用於協作科學推理和問題解決。這些系統要求代理在共享約束下協調，例如 GPU 或信用餘額，其中合作行為至關重要。行為經濟學提供了一套豐富的遊戲工具，能夠孤立出不同的合作機制，但尚不清楚模型在這些理想化環境中的行為是否能預測其在現實協作任務中的表現。在這裡，我們對 35 個開放權重的 LLM 在六個行為經濟學遊戲中進行基準測試，並顯示出基於遊戲的合作特徵能穩健地預測 AI-for-Science 任務中的下游表現，這些任務中 LLM 代理團隊協同分析數據、構建模型並在共享預算約束下產出科學報告。有效協調遊戲並投資於乘法團隊生產（而非貪婪策略）的模型，在準確性、質量和完成度三個結果中產出更好的科學報告。這些關聯在控制多個因素後仍然成立，表明合作傾向是 LLM 的一個獨特且可測量的特性，無法簡化為一般能力。因此，我們的行為遊戲框架提供了一種快速且廉價的診斷方法，用於在成本高昂的多代理部署之前篩選合作適應性。
2604.20658.author: Shivani Kumar et.al.
2604.20658.authors: Shivani Kumar, Adarsh Bharathwaj, David Jurgens
